title: Ancylobacter terrae sp. nov. and Ensifer soli sp. nov., two novel bacterial species isolated from paddy soil.
author_string: Lu, Xie-Feng, Gao, Peng, Zhang, Shu-Yi, Jiang, Hui-Yuan, Liu, Guo-Hong et al.
journal_full: International journal of systematic and evolutionary microbiology
abstract: Two novel aerobic, rod-shaped, non-motile and white-coloured bacterial strains, designated as sgz301288T and sgz301302T, were isolated from paddy soil in Fujian Province. 16S rRNA gene phylogenetic analysis revealed that strain sgz301288T exhibited 97.2% similarity with Ancylobacter moscoviensis KCTC 62336T, while strain sgz301302T had the highest 16S rRNA gene sequence similarity to Ciceribacter azotifigens KACC 19080T (97.4%) and Ensifer terangae LMG 7834T (97.0%). Average nucleotide identity (ANI) and digital DNA-DNA hybridization (dDDH) values between sgz301288T and A. moscoviensis KCTC 62336T were 79.5% and 22.9%, respectively. For strain sgz301302T, ANI and dDDH values were 76.7% and 21.5% with E. terangae LMG 7834T and 75.9% and 20.8% with C. azotifigens KACC 19080T. Phylogenomic analysis further supported strain sgz301288T belonging to Ancylobacter, whereas sgz301302T clustered within Ensifer, distinct from Ciceribacter. The DNA G+C contents of sgz301288T and sgz301302T were 68.4% and 66.5 %, respectively. Strain sgz301288T grew at 20-30 °C (optimum 30 °C) and pH 6.5-9.5 (optimum 7.0) and tolerated up to 0-1.0% NaCl, while strain sgz301302T grew at 20-37 °C (optimum 30 °C), pH 5.0-9.5 (optimum 7.0) and 0-1.5 % NaCl. Both strains contained ubiquinone-10 as the predominant respiratory quinone. The main fatty acids were iso-C17 : 0 3-OH and summed feature 8 (C18 : 1  ω7c and/or C18 : 1  ω6c) for sgz301288T and C16 : 0 and summed feature 8 for sgz301302T. Based on the polyphasic evidence, the proposed names of the two novel species are Ancylobacter terrae sp. nov. (type strain sgz301288T=MCCC 1K09301T=KCTC 8858T) and Ensifer soli sp. nov. (type strain sgz301302T=MCCC 1K09182T=KCTC 8857T), respectively.
